text: In Agile methodology, a project is broken into small, manageable cycles called sprints, usually lasting two to four weeks. Each sprint begins with a planning session where the team selects tasks from a backlog, a prioritized list of work items. These tasks are often written as user stories, simple descriptions of a feature from the end user's perspective. Every day, the team holds a brief standup meeting to discuss progress and challenges. After each sprint, the team reviews the work and adjusts priorities, following the principle of iterative development. This continuous cycle helps teams adapt quickly to change and deliver value faster.
